mnemonics: STP - Short Term, Poor: Remember that short-term memory is limited to about 7±2 items (the 'Magical Number Seven') for a brief duration (~30 seconds) without rehearsal.
keyPoints: Human working memory has a limited capacity, often cited as 7±2 items.
Information in short-term memory decays quickly (~15-30 seconds) without rehearsal.
Cognitive load theory suggests that interfaces must be designed to minimize memory burden to be effective.
commonMistakes: Designing interfaces that require users to remember information from one screen to another.
Overloading users with too many options or pieces of information at once, exceeding the '7±2' rule.
